items.headline: Why Did Albemarle (NYSE:ALB) Gain Market Attention This Week? - Kalkine Media
items.snippet: Albemarle moved into the current market conversation after the company announced a quarterly common stock dividend as lithium and specialty materials remained in focus.

items.headline: Will ALB's Expansion Actions Power Stronger Sales Volumes?
items.snippet: Albemarle Corporation ALB is strategically executing its projects aimed at boosting its global lithium conversion capacity. Higher sales volumes (up 14% year over year) in its Energy Storage unit in Q1.

items.headline: Top Stock Picks for Week of July 13, 2026
items.snippet: Albemarle Corporation (ALB) is a premier specialty chemicals company with leading positions in attractive end markets globally. Earnings estimates for Q2 2026 have been going up over the past month.

items.headline: Albemarle Stock Loses 25% in a Month: What Should Investors Do Now?
items.snippet: Albemarle Corporation's ALB shares have tumbled 25.5% in the past month. Falling lithium market prices are weighing on the ALB stock lately amid slowing EV demand in China, an inventory glut, and prospects of increased supply from mine restarts.

items.headline: Lithium Is on the Rise Again. Does That Make Albemarle a Buy?
items.snippet: The price of lithium is bouncing back. After lithium prices collapsed from their 2022 peaks, the market finally found its floor in late 2024 and has staged a resilient year-to-date rebound. Analysts project a 4% global lithium supply deficit for 2026.

items.headline: Albemarle, the biggest beneficiary of expanding lithium demand... Investment opinion 'Buy' - Citi
items.snippet: Citi has raised its investment opinion on Albemarle (ALB) from 'High Risk' to 'Buy'. The target price remains at $225, citing underestimated growth potential despite recent weakness in spot lithium prices.
